Descriptive Statistics Presentation

Libraries

library(tidyverse)

Loading required package: ggpubr
library(table1)

    myeloma
library(dplyr)

Data Loading

heart2 <- read.csv("data/heart2.csv")

Making table 1

Adding labeled factor versions of certain variables (for table making)

# DIAB statuses/types
heart2$DIAB <- ifelse(is.na(heart2$DIAB), 
                           0, 
                           heart2$DIAB) # making NA become 0s
# heart2$DIAB2 <- factor(heart2$DIAB, 
#                             levels = c(0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 998),
#                             labels = c("Not Reported", "No", "Type I", "Type II", "Type Other", "Type Unknown", "Status Unknown"), 
#                             ordered=TRUE)

heart2 <- heart2 %>%
  mutate(DIAB2 = case_when(DIAB == 1 ~ "No",
      DIAB %in% c(2, 3, 4, 5) ~ "Yes",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    DIAB2 = factor(
      DIAB2,
      levels = c("No", "Yes")))

# Gender
heart2$GENDER2 <- factor(heart2$GENDER, levels = c("F", "M"),
                              labels = c("Female", "Male"),
                              ordered = TRUE)

# # ETHCAT
# heart2$ETHCAT2 <- factor(heart2$ETHCAT, 
#                               levels = c(1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 998),
#                               labels = c("White", "Black", "Hispanic", "Asian", "AI/AN", "Hawaiian/Pacific-Islander", "Multiracial", "Unknown"))
heart2 <- heart2 %>%
  mutate(ETHCAT2 = case_when(
      ETHCAT == 1 ~ "White",
      ETHCAT == 2 ~ "Black",
      ETHCAT == 4 ~ "Hispanic",
      ETHCAT == 5 ~ "Asian",
      ETHCAT %in% c(6, 7, 9, 998) ~ "Other",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    ETHCAT2 = factor(ETHCAT2,
      levels = c("White", "Black", "Hispanic", "Asian", "Other")))

# EXCEPTION
heart2$EXCEPTION2 <- factor(heart2$EXCEPTION, 
                                 levels = c(0, 1),
                                 labels = c("No Exception", "Exception"))
# ESCALATED
heart2$ESCALATED2 <- factor(heart2$ESCALATED, levels = c(0, 1),
                           labels = c("Stable", "Esclatated"))

Descriptive Statistics Table

label(heart2$INIT_AGE) <- "Age at Listing"
label(heart2$INIT_BMI_NEW) <- "BMI at Listing"
label(heart2$GENDER2) <- "Gender"
label(heart2$ETHCAT2) <- "Race/Ethnicity"
label(heart2$BTYPE_REC) <- "Blood Type"
label(heart2$EXCEPTION2) <- "Exception Status"
label(heart2$DIAB2) <- "Diabetes"
label(heart2$ESCALATED2) <- "Escalated Status"

# The Actual Table for Descriptive Statistics
table1(
  ~ INIT_AGE +
    INIT_BMI_NEW +
    GENDER2 +
    ETHCAT2 +
    BTYPE_REC +
    EXCEPTION2 +
    DIAB2 |
    ESCALATED2,
  data = heart2,
  caption = "Table 1. Descriptive Statistics by Escalation Status")

AGE

ggplot(heart2, aes(INIT_AGE)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(title = "Distribution of Age at Initial Listing",
       ylab = "Count") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,800))
Ignoring unknown labels:
• ylab : "Count"
ggplot(heart2, aes(y = INIT_AGE)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "grey") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of Age at Initial Listing") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(18,80), breaks=seq(20,80,10))

BMI

ggplot(heart2, aes(INIT_BMI_NEW)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(title = "Distribution of BMI at Listing",
       y="Count") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,50)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,800))
Warning: Removed 2 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).
Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_bar()`).
ggplot(heart2, aes(y = INIT_BMI_NEW)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of BMI at Listing") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,50))
Warning: Removed 2 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_boxplot()`).

Gender

ggplot(heart2, aes(GENDER2, fill = GENDER2)) +
  geom_bar(color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Female" = "maroon",
                               "Male" = "lightblue")) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Patients grouped by Gender")
# proportions
ggplot(heart2, aes(x = GENDER2, fill = GENDER2)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = after_stat(count / sum(count))), color = "black") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits=c(0,1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Female" = "maroon",
                               "Male" = "lightblue")) +
  geom_text(aes(y = after_stat(count / sum(count)),
      label = scales::percent(after_stat(count / sum(count)), accuracy = 0.1)),
    stat = "count",
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(title = "Patients grouped by Gender",
    x = "Gender",
    y = "Proportion")
ggplot(heart2, aes(ETHCAT2, fill = ETHCAT2)) +
  geom_bar() +
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Patients grouped by Race/Ethnicity")
# props
ggplot(heart2, aes(x = ETHCAT2, fill = ETHCAT2)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = after_stat(count / sum(count)))) +
  geom_text(aes(y = after_stat(count / sum(count)),
      label = scales::percent(after_stat(count / sum(count)), accuracy = 0.1)),
    stat = "count",
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits=c(0,1)) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(title = "Distribution of Patients by Race/Ethnicity",
    x = "Race/Ethnicity",
    y = "Proportion")

Diabetes

ggplot(heart2, aes(DIAB2)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(title = "Patients grouped by Diabetes status")
# props
ggplot(heart2, aes(x = DIAB2)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = after_stat(count / sum(count))),
           fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(y = after_stat(count / sum(count)),
      label = scales::percent(after_stat(count / sum(count)), accuracy = 0.1)),
    stat = "count",
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent,
                     limits=c(0,1),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(title = "Proportion of Patients by Diabetes Status",
    x = "Diabetes Status",
    y = "Proportion")
